title: Diffraction Principles
description: Comprehend the basic principle of diffraction and observe the diffraction pattern in various cases.

title: Interference & Polarisation
description: Illustrate the concepts of interference and polarisation and their practical applications.

title: Crystallography
description: Understand the space lattices and crystal symmetry in solid state physics.

title: Quantum Mechanics Foundations
description: Summarize the fundamental concepts of quantum mechanics and wave theory.

title: Dielectric Materials
description: Explore the properties and applications of dielectric materials in electrical systems.

title: Magnetic Materials
description: Understand the behavior and classification of magnetic materials.

title: Semiconductor Physics
description: Demonstrate the conduction process of charge carriers in semiconductors.

title: P-N Junctions
description: Study the behavior and applications of p-n junction diodes.

title: Advanced Physics Applications
description: Apply physics principles to solve real-world engineering problems.

title: Molecular Orbital Theory
description: Illustrate the Molecular Orbital diagrams for simple diatomic and polyatomic molecules.

title: Superconductors & Semiconductors
description: Identify the applications of superconductors and semiconductors in modern tech.

title: Nanomaterials
description: Explore the synthesis and properties of nano materials for engineering.

title: Spectroscopy Techniques
description: Explain the working of spectroscopy for chemical analysis.

title: Chromatography Methods
description: Understand the principles of chromatography in separating mixtures.

title: Polymers & Fibres
description: Explain the properties and applications of industrial polymers and fibres.

title: Electrochemical Titrations
description: Estimate analytes using potentiometric and conductometric titrations.

title: Electrochemical Cells
description: Describe the construction and working of electrochemical cells and batteries.

title: Chemical Engineering Practice
description: Integrate chemical principles into engineering design and safety.

title: Water Treatment
description: Assess the quality of water and implement effective treatment methods.

title: Electrochemical Potentials
description: Estimate the potentials for various electrochemical cells and systems.

title: Corrosion Factors
description: Describe factors influencing corrosion in metals and materials.

title: Corrosion Prevention
description: Implement modern prevention methods to combat material degradation.

title: Polymers & Fuels
description: Illustrate the types of polymers and industrial fuels with their applications.

title: Colloids & Micelles
description: Describe the concepts of colloids, micelles, and their stability.

title: Refractory Materials
description: Explain the uses and properties of refractory materials in high-temp apps.

title: Lubricants & Cements
description: Understand the selection and use of lubricants and construction cements.

description: Identify the nature of quadratic forms using matrix theory.

title: Mean Value Theorems
description: Apply Mean value theorems to solve complex single variable problems.

description: Make use of partial derivatives to solve multivariable calculus problems.

description: Use multivariable calculus for finding maxima and minima of functions.

title: Multiple Integrals
description: Utilize double and triple integrals to find area and volume of solids.

title: Applied Calculus
description: Relate calculus principles to real-world engineering simulations.

title: First Order ODEs
description: Model and solve first order differential equations for engineering.

title: Higher Order ODEs
description: Apply first and higher order differential equations in system modeling.

title: Initial Value Problems
description: Solve higher order differential equations with specific initial conditions.

title: PDE Applications
description: Understand the use of PDEs in modeling physical phenomena.

title: Vector Differentiation
description: Master the concepts of vector differentiation in multivariable space.

title: Vector Calculus Theorems
description: Apply vector calculus for work, circulation, and flux calculations.

title: Integral Theorems
description: Solve complex problems using Green's, Stokes', and Gauss's theorems.

title: Mathematical Modeling
description: Synthesize DE and Vector Calculus for comprehensive system modeling.

title: DC Circuit Analysis
description: Demonstrate electrical parameters and laws of DC circuits.

title: AC Circuit Analysis
description: Understand the behavior of passive components in AC circuits.

title: Electrical Machines
description: Illustrate the operation of DC and AC electrical machines.

title: Power Plants
description: Understand the working and operation of various types of power plants.

title: Electrical Safety
description: Summarize electrical measurements and essential safety measures.

title: Semiconductor Devices
description: Explain the operation of semiconductor devices and their characteristics.

title: Rectifier Systems
description: Illustrate the operation of half-wave and full-wave rectifiers.

title: Number Systems
description: Master number systems and binary codes for digital logic.

title: Digital Logic Gates
description: Illustrate combinational circuits using basic and universal logic gates.

title: Civil Engineering Basics
description: Describe basics of civil engineering streams and water resources.

title: Surveying Concepts

title: Infrastructure Engineering

title: Engineering Materials

title: Manufacturing Processes

description: Understand basic concepts of thermodynamics and thermal systems.

title: Power Generation
description: Explain the working principles of thermal and hydro power plants.

title: Power Transmission

description: Understand basic configurations and applications of industrial robots.

description: Illustrate algorithms for systematic programming and problem solving.

title: Modular Programming
description: Develop modular program aspects for tackling complex problems.

description: Develop robust programs that use file systems for persistent data.

description: Synthesize all drawing skills for a comprehensive engineering design.

title: Active Listening
description: Understand context and specific information from dialogues and talks.

title: Verbal Communication
description: Speak clearly using appropriate discourse markers in group discussions.

title: Literary Appreciation
description: Comprehend and appreciate various literary texts and styles.

title: Summary Writing
description: Write concise summaries and effective essays on diverse topics.

title: Professional Writing
description: Craft professional letters, e-mails, and modern resumes.

title: Grammatical Mastery
description: Apply grammatical structures accurately in professional settings.

title: Vocabulary Expansion
description: Improve communicative competence with an enhanced English vocabulary.

title: Presentation Skills
description: Develop skills for clear and persuasive oral presentations.

title: Collaborative English
description: Engage in effective collaborative communication in a diverse environment.

title: Force Systems
description: Compute the resultant force in concurrent and coplanar force systems.

title: Equilibrium Analysis
description: Analyze equilibrium conditions for different force systems.

description: Understand the impact of friction on mechanical systems and equilibrium.

title: Linked List Design
